title: iris pseudopumila subsp. gozoensis
intro: Iris pseudopumila subsp. gozoensis, also known as the Gozitan iris, is a subspecies of the Iris pseudopumila plant native to the island of Gozo in Malta. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of the Gozitan iris include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat eye and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Iris pseudopumila subsp. gozoensis typically involve drying and infusing the flowers and leaves in water, or using them in tinctures and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
